config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should routinely take priority over the rights of the individual, because once individual rights become negotiable, it is too easy for governments or majorities to abuse that power. In my view, society works best when basic rights are protected first, and collective interests are pursued within those limits. From my perspective, especially living in a country where people often rely on community but also need protection from arbitrary authority, the balance should favor the individual unless there is a very strong and clearly justified public necessity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika hak individu dibuat terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama “kepentingan umum,” hasilnya sering penyalahgunaan oleh negara atau mayoritas yang merasa paling benar. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan batasan yang ketat, bukan dengan menganggap hak dasar sebagai hal yang bisa dinegosiasikan kapan saja. Kalau ada keadaan darurat yang benar-benar kuat, pembatasan bisa dibenarkan, tetapi beban pembuktiannya harus sangat tinggi.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. In Indonesia, I have seen how appeals to “public interest” can be used to justify pressure from authorities or the majority, and that makes me cautious about giving society the upper hand by default. Collective goals matter, but they should be pursued within firm limits, because once basic rights become optional, the people with the least power are usually the ones who suffer first. A society is healthier when it protects individual rights first and only restricts them in rare, clearly necessary cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa begitu “kepentingan umum” dipakai sebagai alasan default, negara dan mayoritas bisa terlalu mudah menekan kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan kebebasan beragama atas nama ketertiban. Kepentingan masyarakat memang nyata, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak boleh dikorbankan sembarangan, kecuali dalam keadaan darurat yang benar-benar sempit dan teruji. Kalau tidak, yang paling lemah biasanya justru membayar harga paling mahal.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how “public interest” can be used to pressure ordinary people, so I do not trust that phrase when it is treated as a blank check for authorities or the majority. Society matters, but it should be built by protecting basic rights first, because once those rights are easy to override, the people with the least power are the first to lose. I would only accept limiting individual rights in narrow, clearly necessary cases, not as a default principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat sejarah kami berkali-kali menunjukkan bahwa ketika “kepentingan umum” dijadikan alasan default, pemerintah atau mayoritas bisa dengan mudah melampaui batas, terutama pada kebebasan berbicara, privasi, dan beragama. Saya tidak menolak kepentingan kolektif, tetapi itu harus dicapai tanpa menjadikan hak dasar sebagai sesuatu yang bisa dikorbankan sesuka hati. Pengecualian hanya masuk akal dalam keadaan darurat yang benar-benar sempit, terukur, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
